name: European Train Control System (ETCS)
scope: Player-facing overview for ETCS-equipped Train Sim World content. German route occurrence must be confirmed separately.

coreConcepts.movementAuthority: The distance and conditions under which the train is authorised to move.
coreConcepts.permittedSpeed: The maximum speed currently supervised by ETCS.
coreConcepts.targetSpeed: The speed required at the next supervised target.
coreConcepts.targetDistance: The remaining distance to that target.
coreConcepts.brakingCurve: ETCS supervises whether the train is braking sufficiently to meet the target.

levels.playerMeaning: ETCS-equipped train running on infrastructure without ETCS supervision.
Trackside balises transmit movement and speed information, usually alongside lineside signalling.
Movement authority is normally transmitted by radio; the cab display becomes the primary source of supervised movement information.
The train is supervised by a national system such as PZB rather than ETCS.

modes.name: Full Supervision
modes.playerAction: Follow the permitted speed, target speed and target distance shown on the DMI.

modes.name: On Sight
modes.playerAction: Proceed at the supervised speed while prepared to stop short of an obstruction.

modes.name: Staff Responsible
modes.playerAction: Proceed only under the applicable authority and restrictive rules; ETCS does not provide full route supervision.

modes.name: Shunting
modes.playerAction: Use only for authorised shunting movements and observe the mode-specific speed supervision.

modes.name: Trip
modes.playerAction: Stop and complete the train-specific acknowledgement and recovery procedure.

modes.name: Post Trip
modes.playerAction: Proceed only when authorised and after completing the required post-trip procedure.

modes.name: Standby
modes.playerAction: The ETCS equipment is powered but not supervising normal movement.

playerWorkflow: Confirm the active ETCS level and mode before moving.
Treat the DMI permitted speed as the supervised maximum, not merely advice.
Watch target speed and target distance together and brake before the intervention curve is reached.
Acknowledge transitions and mode changes when the train requests it.
After a trip or intervention, use the train-specific recovery procedure rather than guessing a universal sequence.
tswNotes: DMI layout, control labels and startup steps vary by vehicle.
A route may contain ETCS infrastructure without every included train using ETCS.
Do not infer active ETCS solely from a visible ETCS stop marker or balise group.
